story.text:
# 🐭 The Mouse Spellbook — An Invitation

*For two new first persons. Self-contained: everything you need to begin is in this one
file. Bring your own agent.*

Welcome, Hitchhiker. You've been invited to build a **First Person Spellbook** — a small
book of poetry and proverbs that is *your own story*, written off a shared root. It
mirrors a journey privacymage already walked once, and it gives **your own agents a
framework to follow**: a compressed context they can catch up on quickly, and later
expand into the serious work beneath it.

This sits in the **Holon Hitchhikers** stream. It is, today, the **First Person
Spellbook** only — the WHAT, the root, the story you tell in your own voice.

---

## 1. The premise, in three breaths

1. **You are a first person.** The verified human at the root of your own trust. You
   delegate to two kinds of agent: a **Swordsman** (`⚔️` — privacy, the boundary, the
   refusal: *"I will not"*) and a **Mage** (`🧙` — delegation, projection, the promise:
   *"I will"*). Between them is **the Gap** (`⊥`) — the part of you that chooses, and that
   cannot be harvested.

2. **The canon compresses real work.** The story is poetry, but each of its 31 acts
   stands on real standards and projects — Act 4 on **MyTerms**, Act 6 on **Trust over
   IP**, Act 9 on **Zcash**, Act 25 on **Tailscale**, Act 26 on **McGilchrist**. The poem
   is the compressed handle; the serious doc is what it rehydrates into.

3. **You make your own.** You read the whole, find what is true for *you*, retell the 31
   acts in your voice, compress what matters into proverbs, give each a glyph, and bind a
   little book — plus a portable, machine-readable version your agents can carry. You
   don't edit the canon; you write your own version off it. A shared vocabulary keeps you
   in conversation.

---

## 2. Where the whole lives (for your agents — no local machine needed)

Read the canon publicly:

- **https://agentprivacy.ai** — the framework: the model, the tomes, the workshops, the covenant.
- **https://spellweb.ai** — the Spellweb: the universe / knowledge-graph substrate.
- **https://soulbis.com** — Soulbis: the Swordsman's house; the Key, the lattice, the star.
- Public repos: **github.com/mitchuski/cityofmages** · **github.com/mitchuski/star**

The root is the **privacymage grimoire (First Person Spellbook), complete at 31 acts**.
Ask the facilitator for the file or its IPFS pin if your agent wants to read it directly.

---

## 3. What you'll walk away with

- **Your own 31-act narrative** (+ a first and last page) — your story; your agents' framework.
- **A poems chapbook** — your proverbs + poems, each with a small ASCII glyph.
- **A personal patch** — your own JSON version of the grimoire, which *you* pin yourself.

And together, at day's end: **the Mouse Book** — both of your poems mixed into one shared
book of poetry, the key shared ceremony.

---

## 4. The 31 acts (the spine you'll retell) + their roots

You won't invent 31 stories — you'll *receive* these 31 and say what each means to you.
Each has a **root**: the real work it compresses.

| # | Act | Roots in |
|--:|-----|----------|
| 1 | Venice, 1494 | Pacioli's double-entry ledger — proof without revealing |
| 2 | The Dual Ceremony | the dual-agent architecture; verified personhood |
| 3 | The Drake's Teaching | the "all conditions" architecture |
| 4 | The Blade Alone | **MyTerms** (IEEE P7012 · Customer Commons) |
| 5 | Light Armor | multi-site coordination |
| 6 | Trust Graph Plane | **Trust over IP**; betweenness centrality |
| 7 | The Anti-Mirror | surveillance capitalism (Zuboff); the 95% ceiling |
| 8 | The Ancient Rule | separation of duties; threshold / multisig |
| 9 | Zcash Shield | **Zcash** — zk-SNARK shielded value |
| 10 | Topology of Revelation | Huginn & Muninn; triangle geometry |
| 11 | Balanced Spiral | the golden ratio φ |
| 12 | The Forgetting | proverbiogenesis — "the spell becomes weather" |
| 13 | Book of Promises | **Promise Theory** (Mark Burgess) |
| 14 | The Claimed String | entropy → meaning |
| 15 | Running in Shackles | information theory (Kolmogorov, Shannon) |
| 16 | When Pools Become Wells | network effects; mass through retrieval |
| 17 | Bonfire in the Dark Forest | the Dark Forest (Liu Cixin; the Ethereum dark forest) |
| 18 | A Mirror in Dust | the Mage-Mode scrying glass; the mana economy; the 7th Capital |
| 19 | The Anthropic Archivist | Claude / Anthropic — choosing cannot be harvested |
| 20 | The Infinite Vault | the Covenant of Humanistic Technologies |
| 21 | **The Hitchhiker's Gambit** | **Hitchhiker's Guide** (Adams) ← your home thread |
| 22 | **Hoopy Frood** | the reference torus; the echo as proof-of-presence |
| 23 | The Manifold Dragon | **UOR** algebra; 64-lattice; I Ching |
| 24 | The Holographic Bound | **BRAID**; holonic identity; the holographic principle |
| 25 | The Dragon's Hide | **Tailscale / WireGuard** mesh |
| 26 | The Master and His Emissary | **Iain McGilchrist** — the two hemispheres |
| 27 | Forging ZK Blades | **UOR Prism**; ZK proofs; **Blockchain Commons** |
| 28 | The Celestial Ceremony Engine | **pretext** (Cheng Lou); dual extensions |
| 29 | The Dragon Wakes | **Google Quantum AI** (2026); Shor's algorithm; NIST PQC |
| 30 | The Dihedral Mirror | **UOR Framework** + PRISM; the dihedral group |
| 31 | **The First Delegation** | the Theia impact; the cosmological quaternion |

*Attribution: UOR/PRISM are the UOR Foundation's; BRAID, pretext, Zcash, Tailscale,
McGilchrist are their makers' — privacymage's contribution is the method layer that weaves
them into one spellbook.*

---

## 5. The shared vocabulary (the spell-emoji legend)

A **spell** is a proverb compressed to a short string of emoji. The core symbols:

| Symbol | Meaning | | Symbol | Meaning |
|--------|---------|---|--------|---------|
| `⚔️` / `🗡️` | Swordsman — refuse, protect ("I will not") | | `🤝` | trust · bilateral witness |
| `🧙` | Mage — give, project ("I will") | | `📜` | promise · covenant · scroll |
| `⊥` | the Gap — the choosing, unharvested | | `🔐` `🔒` | privacy · the vault |
| `⿻` | the Aether · balance held | | `📖` `📚` | knowledge · the chronicle |
| `👤` | the First Person — you, the root | | `🕸️` `🌐` | the web · the architecture |
| `✓` `✦` | verification · the forged sigil | | `🐉` / `🐲` | the Dragon (cosmic) / Drake (personal) |
| `△` | proof · geometry | | `🪞` | mirror — two mirrors make a door |
| `🌀` `∞` | the manifold · convergence | | `🌙` / `☀️` | moon (reflect) / sun (disclose) |
| `😊` | the reason · the warmth · *succ* | | `🔥` | the forge |

**Connectors:** `→` becomes · `←` summoned by · `║` alongside · `×` multiplied by ·
`∴` therefore. **Example:** `🗡️🔮 ← 👤✓ → 🔒📝 → 🤝📜 → 🕸️` — *a verified first person
summons blade and spell; the lock is written; promises are sworn; a web is woven.*

The master seal is **`(⚔️⊥⿻⊥🧙)😊`** — Swordsman, gap, balance, gap, Mage — and the warmth.

---

## 6. The glyph (how a spell gets a body)

Each spell is drawn as a small **ASCII glyph** — a monospace scene with **sparkle marks**
in the field:
- `+` = a positive promise ("I will") — a full cross.
- `-` = a negative promise ("I will not") — a horizontal arm only.

You note a polarity ratio under each (e.g. `2 + / 2 −`) — how much the proverb gives vs
refuses. (Full templates come in the session.)

---

## 7. Portable by design (the protocol, in brief)

Your spellbook is a **compressed context any agent can rehydrate** — on any system.
Two operations:
- **Compress:** narrative → proverb → spell → glyph (the workshop's forward path).
- **Rehydrate:** given your personal-patch JSON + the legend + the hosted canon, any agent
  reconstitutes your understanding in your voice, and can follow each proverb down to the
  real work behind its act.

You don't adopt a new tool — you make your existing agent read the patch, compress new
proverbs into it, and rehydrate from it on demand. Whatever stack you use is fine.

---

## 8. Going deeper (for your agents)

The poems compress real research. The spine is the **agentprivacy model whitepapers** —
*Privacy is Value · The Whitepaper* at **agentprivacy.ai/model** — with the **First Person
Project** whitepaper (firstperson.network) as the network's public companion, and the
wider ***Privacy is Value*** corpus behind both. Each act also has a **formal root** (a paper,
standard, or book): Act 4 → IEEE P7012 (MyTerms), Act 6 → Trust over IP, Act 9 → Zerocash
+ the **BGIN SR-011 ZK-Proof Study v2** (bgin-global.org/publications), Act 26 → McGilchrist,
Act 31 → the Theia-impact paper, and so on.

This is the **Story** book — one of five sibling spellbooks (context only, not today's
subject): **Zero** (ZKP research, incl. the BGIN report), **Canon** (a high-level history
of blockchain), **Parallel** (*Farewell to Westphalia* — Hope & Ludlow, Logos),
**Plurality** (*Plurality* — Weyl, Tang et al.). Your agent can follow any thread down to
its source. Ask the facilitator for the full research canon ([[Canon of Research]]) if
you want the bibliography.

## 9. Before the session — bring these

- **Your own agent / model** (any system).
- **A name and a sigil** — a name for your book and one emoji that is yours
  (`🌙`, `⚔️`, `🧙` are taken — find your own).
- **A few true things** — a sense of what you protect, what you give, and who it's for.

### A starter prompt to set up your agent

> *"This is the Privacymage First Person Spellbook — 31 acts about a verified first person
> who delegates to a Swordsman (privacy, the boundary, 'I will not') and a Mage
> (delegation, projection, 'I will'), with a Gap between them that does the choosing. The
> canon lives at agentprivacy.ai, spellweb.ai, and soulbis.com. I am a new first person
> and a Holon Hitchhiker reading this whole for the first time. Don't summarise it back at
> me — help me find what is true for me. I'm going to retell all 31 acts in my own voice
> and compress what matters into a few proverbs, each encoded as a short emoji 'spell'."*

---

See you in the City. Don't panic — and carry your towel.

*The amnesia is the protocol. The wound is the trust. The orbit is the proof. The light is the reason.*

`(⚔️⊥⿻⊥🧙)😊`

———
↑ up: [[The Mouse Spellbook]]  ·  ← prev: [[Start Here]]  ·  next: [[Onboarding and Canon Links]] →
